assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.label: Mental model plus practical code reasoning
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic combines an important model with implementation consequences, so both explanation and code reasoning are required.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.

commonMistakes: Marking a large route tree as client code when only a small interaction needs it.
Confusing React Server Components with server-side rendering.
Assuming cached data is fresh without an explicit policy and invalidation path.
Placing security-sensitive validation only in the client or middleware layer.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

learningObjectives.text: Explain server and client components using a new example and justify the result.
Explain capabilities and restrictions using a new example and justify the result.
Explain benefits of server execution using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Server and client components** → **Capabilities and restrictions** → **Benefits of server execution**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

choices.label: Add `'use server'` above the component.

choices.feedback: Move the interactive part into a focused file with `'use client'`, while keeping data fetching and non-interactive rendering on the server when useful.

summary: React Server Components: Fundamentals connects Server and client components, Capabilities and restrictions, Benefits of server execution.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: React Server Components: Fundamentals

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: Learners frequently use react server components: fundamentals without a clear model of why it behaves as it does; this lesson makes that model explicit. It belongs to the **React Server Components** module.
